intro: The Department of City Planning traces its roots to the City Planning Commission, created under the City's 1936 reform charter and seated in 1938 to prepare a master plan and oversee zoning and land use as the city grew. Today DCP is New York's primary land-use agency, supporting the City Planning Commission's review of roughly 450 land-use applications a year and shaping citywide zoning, housing, and neighborhood plans across the five boroughs.
